title: Terraform Providers
learningObjectives: Configure provider blocks with version constraints

theory.content:
What are Providers?

Providers are plugins that implement resource types for specific APIs — AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes, GitHub, etc. The Terraform Registry (registry.terraform.io) hosts hundreds of providers. Each provider has its own lifecycle and version.

Configuring Providers

terraform {
  required_version = ">= 1.13.0"
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 5.0"
    }
    random = {
      source  = "hashicorp/random"
      version = ">= 3.5.0"
    }
  }
}

provider "aws" {
  region = var.aws_region
  # Credentials from env: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
  # or from ~/.aws/credentials
}

Version Constraints

ConstraintMeaning
= 5.0.0Exactly 5.0.0
!= 5.0.1Not 5.0.1
>= 5.0, < 6.0Any 5.x
~> 5.0Pessimistic: >= 5.0, < 6.0
~> 5.0.0Pessimistic: >= 5.0.0, < 5.1.0

Provider Aliases (Multiple Regions)

provider "aws" {
  region = "us-east-1"
}

provider "aws" {
  alias  = "west"
  region = "us-west-2"
}

resource "aws_instance" "west_server" {
  provider      = aws.west
  ami           = "ami-0123456789abcdef0"
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
}

Provider Lock File

The .terraform.lock.hcl file locks provider versions and checksums. Commit this file to version control to ensure consistent provider versions across team members and CI/CD.
